messages.subject: [R-gui] Tinn-R 1.18.5.9 released
messages.body_plain:
Dear Tinn-R user,

We would like to announce that a new version of the Tinn-R for Windows
(1.18.5.9) was released and is available on SourceForge server:
http://sourceforge.net/projects/tinn-r.

Tinn-R is a R script editor under OS Windows.

To see the changes, please, read: 'Help//English/Readme/HTML'.

Shortly it will be available also on SciViews server: http://www.sciviews.org/Tinn-R

Regards,
Tinn-R team

messages.subject: [R-gui] Information about wxPython
messages.body_plain:
I think that wxPython is a dead project, but Duncan Temple Lang is 
developing a new binding to wxWidgets, called RwxWidgets. I think you 
can google for that, or email him directly.

Michael

Marcelo Damasceno wrote:
> Hello to all,
>
> I wanna know information how to use wxPython, like R packages, IDE's,
> softwares.
> I tried to install RSPython, but can't install because this error:
> ../inst/include/RPythonModule.h:4:20: error: Python.h: File or Directory did
> not find.
>
> I searched information about wxPython in this mailing list, any information
> is in this site:  http://bioinf.wehi.edu.au/folders/james/wxPython/, but
> unfortunately this link is out.
>
> Thanks for any information.
>
>
>   
> ------------------------------------------------------------------------
>
> _______________________________________________
> R-SIG-GUI mailing list
> R-SIG-GUI at stat.math.ethz.ch
> https://stat.ethz.ch/mailman/listinfo/r-sig-gui
>
messages.body_snippet:
I think that wxPython is a dead project, but Duncan Temple Lang is 
developing a new binding to wxWidgets, called RwxWidgets. I think you 
can google for that, or email him directly.

Michael

Marcelo

messages.subject: [R-gui] Information about wxPython
messages.body_plain:
Hi Marcelo.

  As for wxWidgets, I am working on creating a direct interface
between R and wxWidget in the RwxWidgets package.  I have done some
of the important widgets by hand and expect to complete the
automated bindings for the entire wxWidgets classes and routines
in the next 2-3 weeks.  It is close, but I just need to find time.



  The installation problem with RSPython indicates that you
probably don't have the development facilities for using python,
i.e. the header files and libraries.  You probably need to install
them via a package such as python-devel on a linux box
or build python from source.



Marcelo Damasceno wrote:
> Hello to all,
> 
> I wanna know information how to use wxPython, like R packages, IDE's,
> softwares.
> I tried to install RSPython, but can't install because this error:
> ../inst/include/RPythonModule.h:4:20: error: Python.h: File or Directory did
> not find.
> 
> I searched information about wxPython in this mailing list, any information
> is in this site:  http://bioinf.wehi.edu.au/folders/james/wxPython/, but
> unfortunately this link is out.
> 
> Thanks for any information.
> 
> 
> -- 
> Marcelo Damasceno de Melo
> Graduando em Ci?ncia da Computa??o
> Departamento de Tecnologia da Informa??o - TCI
> Universidade Federal de Alagoas - UFAL

messages.subject: [R-gui] cairoDevice 1.2 released
messages.body_plain:
Just to let the GUI guys know,

I've managed to get cairoDevice on CRAN. The new version (1.2) fixes 
many bugs and adds support for getGraphicsEvent() so you can make more 
interactive graphics.

For those not familiar with cairoDevice, it is an antialiased R graphics 
device based on the cairo graphics library. It is embeddable into 
RGtk2-based GUIs.

Have fun,
Michael

messages.subject: [R-gui] Tinn-R 1.18.6.1 released
messages.body_plain:
Dear Tinn-R user,

We would like to announce that a new version of the Tinn-R for Windows
(1.18.6.1) was released and is available on SourceForge server:
http://sourceforge.net/projects/tinn-r

Tinn-R is a R script editor under OS Windows.

To see the changes, please, read: 'Help//English/Readme/HTML'.

Shortly it will be available also on SciViews server: http://www.sciviews.org/Tinn-R

Changes:
1.18.6.1 (Sep/28/2006)

  - Due to some problems detected in the file 'default.bin'
    associated with the version 1.18.6.0, it was updated. So,
    it is strongly recommended that, before to use this new
    version, you remove manually this file. To help you to
    discovery where Tinn-R stores all the ini files, a new
    option was added to the main menu 'Tools' named
    'Ini files'. The 'default.bin' file is located in the
    sub-folder shortcuts. So, go to the folder and delete
    this file, no more. Sorry for the inconvenience.

Regards,
Tinn-R team

messages.subject: [R-gui] Tinn-R 1.18.6.3 released
messages.body_plain:
Dear Tinn-R user,

We would like to announce that a new version of the Tinn-R for Windows
(1.18.6.3) was released and is available on SourceForge server:
http://sourceforge.net/projects/tinn-r

Tinn-R is a R script editor under OS Windows.

To see the changes, please, read: 'Help//English/Readme/HTML'.

Shortly it will be available also on SciViews server: http://www.sciviews.org/Tinn-R

Changes:

1.18.6.3 (Sep/29/2006)

  - The Shortcuts customization interface was reworked, we think it is now more
    user friendly.
  - In the interface above you have a new button that enables you to restore the
    main default Tinn-R shortcuts.

Regards,
Tinn-R team
